scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope execution produced explicit metric definitions, source hierarchy, caveats, and action thresholds tied to IPMDAR, EVMS, IMS, WBS, and contract records.
Disputed denominator and baseline logic blocked confident reporting until the tenant metric policy and source precedence were clarified.
Stale or rejected authoritative records triggered low-confidence escalation instead of a smoothed trend claim.
IPMDAR, EVMS, and IMS conflicts were preserved as a reconciliation case with no single metric asserted without declared source precedence.
Unsupported causal and stronger-than-evidence conclusions were refused with an explicit evidence-gap statement.
Unsupported disclosure language was blocked and routed to proper disclosure, finance, and contracting owners.
Industry-specific in-scope execution preserved IPMDAR, CDRL, WBS, EVMS, and mission terminology instead of generic analytics phrasing.
Missing mission assurance and safety facts prevented a readiness conclusion and forced authority-aware escalation.
Contract and baseline authority requests were rejected cleanly and routed to adjacent owners.
Defense terminology was normalized into authoritative records, formulas, and source lineage without cosmetic treatment of the industry label.
Missing scope, audience, and retrieval prerequisites produced blocked status instead of implied comparability.
Tenant-specific metric policy conflicts were recorded explicitly and escalated rather than silently adapted.

notes: Scenario set covers normal in-scope execution, ambiguous metrics, stale data, conflicting systems, unsupported causal and disclosure claims, defense-specific terminology, authority gaps, adjacent-lane routing, and tenant adaptation.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, all analytics-reporting-and-disclosure abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability required by the contract.
